A special mention goes to Nilambhar for his Dosa and Vijaya Raju for his Hot Filter coffee, Athith, Shreyas, along with the rest of the team on duty. Their attentiveness, warmth, and prompt service made my halt incredibly comfortable and pleasant. The wash rooms are also neatly maintained. It is rare to see such dedicated staff who go out of their way to make guests feel welcome.
Thank you for the great hospitality, and keep up the fantastic work I look forward to visiting again.

In the pulsating heart of Bengaluru's Kempegowda International Airport Terminal 1, the lounge emerges as a veritable sanctuary of sophistication and superlative service a luminous beacon amid the frenzy of air travel. From the moment one steps through its portals, an aura of refined opulence envelops the senses: plush seating cradling weary travelers like a gentle embrace, ambient lighting that dances with subtle artistry, and a culinary symphony that tantalizes with gourmet precision. This is not merely a lounge; it is an exalted interlude, where every detail whispers excellence and elevates the mundane to the magnificent.
Yet, the true luminary of this stellar experience is the virtuoso bartender, Anoop Singh, whose mastery behind the bar transcends craftsmanship into the realm of alchemy. With the finesse of a maestro conducting a symphony, Anoop orchestrates libations that are nothing short of revelatory each mocktail a harmonious fusion of flavors, impeccably balanced and presented with theatrical flair. His encyclopedic knowledge of spirits, paired with an intuitive prescience for personalized preferences, transforms a simple drink into a bespoke masterpiece. Engaging with the warmth of a cherished host and the precision of a sommelier extraordinaire, Anoop elevates hospitality to an art form, ensuring every guest departs not just refreshed, but profoundly invigorated.
This lounge, under Anoop Singh's aegis, redefines airport indulgence. A resounding five-star accolade unreservedly recommended for discerning travelers seeking transcendence in transit.

That said, the check-in experience could be improved. I arrived a little early for check-in, and I was disappointed with the way one of the staff members handled the situation. The interaction felt dismissive, and at one point I felt as though I was being mocked, which was not a pleasant experience.

I would also like to highlight an issue regarding the feedback process. A staff member approached me and requested that I mention specific employees by name in my feedback. While I understand encouraging guests to recognize good service, I had not interacted with the three individuals whose names were suggested to me. Asking guests to provide positive feedback is one thing, but pressuring them to mention specific names is unprofessional and undermines the credibility of the feedback process.

I approached the customer service center to check whether my card was eligible for lounge access. I presented three different cards at the counter, and just by seeing the card, the CSA (customer service assistant) said it was not eligible. Then I approached them a second time after doing some research and presented my international CBA card. They just mentioned that no international physical card is eligible, but a digital card can be. Then I showed multiple digital cards, but the reply was disappointing. Then, I went back and did a simple search about how to access it. I learned that I could download the travel pass and register my card, which is a simple step by step procedure that I believe the CSA would know and could direct me through unless they haven't received proper training on how to deal with customers. After following those steps, I could easily access the QR code, and my card actually has eligibility for two credits per year, which I wouldn't be using.
